Session 145 -- US-272 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 23 TIER 1-B story 1 of 6 unclaimed; TD-040 closure-in-fact records cleanup + spec-required shape-not-literal regex test). TWO artifacts in scope.filesToTouch + ONE inbox note (informational). tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py UPDATE: added test_releaseVersionFile_holdsValidSemver (file lines 393-410) -- asserts r'^V\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+$' regex on version field + len(description)>0 non-empty check; mirrors US-269/TD-044 shape-not-literal pattern at tests/server/test_migration_0005_dtc_log.py:160-168 including the sibling-test independence comment style; +import re; mod-history entry; net +24 lines. offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-040-release-version-seed-test-broken-by-sprint19-close-bump.md UPDATE: Status: Resolved + Closed In: commit 57bdda6 + US-272 Rex Session 145 + new Closure section documenting the two-stage closure (Stage 1 = commit 57bdda6 CIO 2026-04-30 22:21 CDT theme-field schema addition that explicitly stated 'closes TD-040 via deletion' but record never updated; Stage 2 = US-272 added explicit shape-not-literal test per Sprint 23 spec acceptance #2) + verification commands. THIRD artifact (informational, standard PM-communication channel): offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us272-spec-divergence-rename-target-absent.md NEW (147 lines) documents the deliberate spec divergence I shipped: spec called for *renaming* test_releaseVersionFile_seedVersionIsV0_18_0 -> test_releaseVersionFile_holdsValidSemver (invariant #3 even said 'count stays same'); pre-flight discovered the rename target was absent (commit 57bdda6 had deleted it 3 days before sprint groom); shipped the new test as ADD not RENAME per CIO standing rule 'PM communication for missing stitching' + filed the inbox note for PM adjudication. Inbox note also names the records-drift pattern explicitly: 4 instances this sprint (TD-040 + I-015 + I-016 + I-017) where code shipped but record stayed open -- candidate for future sprint_lint extension to verify TD/issue reproduction commands at groom time (the cheaper extension is US-274's file-existence check; the more ambitious is reproduction-command-verification). PRE-FLIGHT AUDITS: spec acceptance #1 'rg seedVersionIsV0_18_0|V0\\.18\\.0 tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py' returned ZERO hits for seedVersionIsV0_18_0 (the load-bearing data point); the V0.18.0 hits in the file are intentional test fixtures for parseVersion + validateRelease + composeReleaseRecord (NOT assertions on the deploy file). git log -p -S 'seedVersionIsV0_18_0' confirmed commit 57bdda6 deleted it. VERIFICATION: pre-fix runtime-validation gate per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md -- (a) python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('deploy/RELEASE_VERSION')); d['version']='0.22.0'; open('deploy/RELEASE_VERSION','w').write(json.dumps(d))" + pytest target 1 FAILED with AssertionError: ...must match V<major>.<minor>.<patch>: got '0.22.0'; git checkout deploy/RELEASE_VERSION restored. (b) python -c "...d['description']='';..." + pytest target 1 FAILED with AssertionError: ...must be non-empty; git checkout restored. (c) Post-fix pytest target 1/1 PASS. Both bug classes proven loud at the assertion level. Post-fix targeted: pytest tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py -v 55/55 PASS in 29.27s (was 54; +1 = exactly the new test). Ruff check tests/deploy/test_release_versioning.py -- All checks passed. sprint_lint US-272 OK; 0 errors / 0 warnings across all 9 Sprint 23 stories (cleanest groom sustained through 4 stories now). Full fast suite: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q -- 3901 passed / 1 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1137.14s (18:57). Baseline post-US-277 (Session 144) was 3900 / 1 / 17; delta +1 pass / 0 failure change = exactly the 1 new test + ZERO regressions. The 1 residual failure is PRE-EXISTING on main and OUT OF SCOPE per Refusal Rule 3: tests/lint/test_no_hardcoded_addresses.py B-044 violation in scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py:80,332,333 (already on main; flagged Sessions 139-144 close notes; warrants its own TD or US-281 if not already filed). Sprint-23-own-sprint failures: ZERO. Phantom-path drift broke its 14-session pattern again: scope.filesToTouch listed real existing paths (no silent-substitute needed) -- 14 consecutive sessions broken now since Session 130. PATTERN CARRYFORWARD: rename-target-absent is a deliberate-divergence trigger -- when a spec calls for renaming an existing artifact and the artifact is gone (closed-in-fact by an earlier commit), the right move is shipping the equivalent (add-not-rename) + filing an inbox note. Mirrors US-277's /var/run ownership divergence (Session 144). Refusal Rule 1 'ambiguity = blocker' does NOT apply when the work is functionally clear; Rule 1 applies when spec intent itself is unclear. The contrast pattern: US-272 (closure-in-fact pre-existed -> ship spec-equivalent) vs US-277 (functional correctness contradicted spec invariant -> ship functionally-correct + flag the spec divergence). Both file inbox notes for PM adjudication; both ship passes:true. PATTERN DISCOVERED: dual-mutation runtime-validation gate for shape + non-empty composite assertions -- mutate once per assertion clause and verify each clause fires independently. The two distinct AssertionError messages prove the test catches both bug classes with explicit gates, not as an accidental side-effect of one assertion masking the other. PATTERN OBSERVED: spec-acceptance pre-flight commands can return ZERO hits and that itself is a finding -- the spec said 'confirm scope is one test method'; zero hits for seedVersionIsV0_18_0 WAS the load-bearing data point that triggered the divergence path. Always interpret pre-flight results, don't just run them. SPRINT 23 STATUS NOW: 4/9 SHIPPED (US-275 + US-276 + US-277 ALL TIER 1-A + US-272 TIER 1-B). Remaining unclaimed 5/9: TIER 1-B US-270 (S; TD-001 TestDataManager pytest collection -- one-line __test__ = False addition; closes the 2 long-standing pytest warnings visible in EVERY fast suite run incl Session 145's tail), US-271 (S; TD-006 .env.example OLLAMA_BASE_URL doc; ~5-min trivial), US-273 (S; I-015 + I-016 + I-017 records-only closure -- mirrors US-272 records-drift pattern; quick), US-278 (S; UPS HAT dropout characteristics doc); TIER 2 US-274 (M; sprint_lint file-existence check -- the durable AI-001 fix for phantom-path drift; biggest-impact-per-medium). Recommended next-iteration pick: US-270 (S; one-line __test__ = False on TestDataManager; closes 2 visible pytest warnings + closes a Sprint-1 long-standing TD; trivial scope; ~5-min job). Alternatives: US-273 (S; records-only I-015/I-016/I-017 closure -- bundles 3 records cleanups in one story; reinforces the records-drift fix pattern just shipped); US-274 (M; the AI-001 fix; biggest sustained-impact return on the phantom-path-drift pattern that has now broken its 14-session run). US-271 (S; .env.example doc) and US-278 (S; UPS HAT doc) are the simplest pure-doc additions if a low-cognitive-load story is preferred.

----- PRIOR SESSION 144 -----
Session 144 -- US-277 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 23 TIER 1-A FINISHER; the 3rd of 3 ladder-discriminator + forensic-gap stories; auto-wires drain-forensics systemd install into deploy-pi.sh + ships Spool's bonus PYTHONPATH fix). THREE artifacts in scope.filesToTouch: deploy/drain-forensics.service UPDATE (+Environment=PYTHONPATH=/home/mcornelison/Projects/Eclipse-01 -- without it Python's automatic sys.path bootstrap only adds the script's parent scripts/ dir not the project root, so `import src.pi.power.*` would silently ImportError and every fire would no-op; Spool live-patched the /etc/systemd/system/ copy mid-Drain-7 but the repo file was unchanged; rewrote the header install-instructions block to point to the new auto-wire + documented /var/run ownership rationale -- ~21 lines added across 2 hunks); deploy/deploy-pi.sh UPDATE (+step_install_drain_forensics_unit function + dispatch wire-up after step_install_eclipse_obd_unit; the function mirrors step_install_journald_persistent + step_install_eclipse_obd_unit pattern with cmp -s idempotency + install -m 644 sync-on-change + daemon-reload-only-when-changed + unconditional `enable --now drain-forensics.timer` for out-of-band-disable recovery; full DRY_RUN-mode branch printing all the would-do operations; +99 lines net); tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py NEW (305 lines / 18 tests across 3 classes -- TestDrainForensicsServicePythonPath x2 + TestDeployPiShDrainForensicsStep x8 + TestDeployPiShDryRunAnnouncesNewStep x7 + 1 standalone bash-syntax-valid test). FOURTH artifact (informational, not in scope.filesToTouch but standard PM-communication channel): offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-03-from-rex-us277-var-run-ownership-divergence.md -- documents one deliberate spec divergence I shipped: invariant #3 + scope.filesToTouch said /var/run/eclipse-obd should be root:root; I shipped mcornelison:mcornelison. Reason: US-276's writer runs inside eclipse-obd.service (User=mcornelison); root:root with default 0755 would block the write and trigger US-276's `except OSError` log+skip path on every tick, leaving the drain_forensics CSV's pd_stage + pd_tick_count columns at the same -1/unknown sentinel that motivated this whole TIER 1-A stack in the first place. The spec parenthetical `(systemd writes there)` likely reflects systemd-tmpfiles mental model (which does run as root); functionally the writer is a user-space service. CIO standing rule `PM communication for missing stitching` covers this case: ship the functionally-correct version + file an inbox note for PM adjudication. test_runtimeDirsOwnedByMcornelison pins the new ownership against the function body so a future revert would surface loudly. PRE-FLIGHT AUDITS: `rg "step_install" deploy/deploy-pi.sh` confirmed existing patterns (step_install_journald_persistent + step_install_eclipse_obd_unit + step_install_rfcomm_bind + step_install_python_deps + step_install_system_deps); `grep -E '^User=|^Group=' deploy/eclipse-obd.service` confirmed eclipse-obd.service runs as User=mcornelison -- the load-bearing fact behind the /var/run ownership decision. VERIFICATION: pre-fix runtime-validation gate per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md -- `git stash push -- deploy/deploy-pi.sh deploy/drain-forensics.service` reverted both file edits (test_drain_forensics_install.py preserved); pytest tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py -v ran 16 failed / 2 passed in 54.89s. The 2 PASSED are negative-invariant / regression-coverage tests (test_dryRunDoesNotCallRealSsh because pre-fix dry-run also did not attempt real SSH; test_deployPiSh_bashSyntaxValid because pre-fix bash syntax was always valid). The 16 failures span every discriminator. `git stash pop` restored. Post-fix targeted: 18/18 PASS in 44.26s. bash -n deploy/deploy-pi.sh -- OK. Full deploy suite: pytest tests/deploy/ -v -- 112 passed in 123.07s (baseline post-US-276 was 94; delta +18 = exactly the new tests + ZERO regressions on the prior 94). Full fast suite: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' -q -- 3900 passed / 1 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 1031.92s (17:11). Baseline post-US-276 (Session 143) was 3882 / 1 / 17; delta +18 pass / 0 failure change = exactly the 18 new tests + ZERO regressions. The 1 residual failure is PRE-EXISTING on main and OUT OF SCOPE per Refusal Rule 3: tests/lint/test_no_hardcoded_addresses.py B-044 violation in scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py:80,332,333 (already on main; flagged Sessions 139-143 close notes). Sprint-23-own-sprint failures: ZERO. ruff check tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py -- All checks passed (one F841 unused-variable mid-implementation correction; auto-fixed before final run -- the assertion logic was identical pre-correction, just had a dead local var I no longer needed). sprint_lint US-277 OK; 0 errors / 0 warnings across all 9 Sprint 23 stories (cleanest groom sustained through ALL 3 TIER 1-A stories). Phantom-path drift broke its 13-session pattern again: scope.filesToTouch listed real existing paths (deploy/drain-forensics.service + deploy/deploy-pi.sh + new tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py). PATTERN CARRYFORWARD: paired-test-strategy where discriminator tests (negative-on-pre-fix, positive-on-post-fix) and regression-coverage tests (positive-on-both, would-go-red-on-future-revert) share a file -- gives you the runtime-validation gate signal AND a maintenance signal in the same place. The 16/2 split is the cleanest version of this pattern I have shipped: pre-fix 16 of 18 fail loudly, post-fix all 18 pass; if a future change reverts ownership or removes the function call, the discriminator tests would re-fail. PATTERN DISCOVERED: when adding a function to a heavily-conventioned bash script (deploy-pi.sh has 5+ existing step_install_* functions), mirroring an existing function's exact shape + variable names + comment-block structure makes review+grep ergonomics dramatically better than inventing a fresh shape; reviewer can diff the new function against the prior one and verify only the specifics changed. PATTERN OBSERVED (carryforward from US-276): re-read stop-conditions BEFORE finalizing the implementation -- here the ownership stop-condition wasn't documented in stopConditions[] but was an invariant violation; my decision to diverge was deliberate-and-documented (inbox note, scopeFenceNote, dedicated test) rather than implicit. Inbox notes are how Ralph routes deploy-side stitching gaps that aren't covered by stop-conditions. SPRINT 23 STATUS NOW: 3/9 SHIPPED (US-275 + US-276 + US-277; ALL 3 TIER 1-A stories DONE; the post-Drain-8 stack is fully wired). TWO artifacts: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py UPDATE (+stateFilePath:Path|None constructor parameter default None to preserve every existing test fixture; +_writeStateFile helper using POSIX atomic-rename via os.replace -- write JSON to <path>.tmp + replace to final; tick() body extracted into _tickBody helper and wrapped in try/finally so the state-file write happens on EVERY exit path -- enabled=False bail / TRIGGER terminal / EXTERNAL+UNKNOWN+vcell_none early returns / normal flow with state transitions; per stop-condition #1 the writer does NOT mkdir the parent dir -- US-277's deploy-pi.sh + systemd-tmpfiles owns /var/run/eclipse-obd/ provisioning -- a missing dir falls through to OSError catch and is logged-and-skipped; mod-history entry US-276 closure 2026-05-02; +73 lines; 876 LOC total). tests/pi/power/test_orchestrator_state_file.py NEW (350 lines / 6 tests across 3 classes -- TestStateFileHappyPath x3 covers reader-required keys pd_stage + pd_tick_count + spec additive fields lastTickTimestamp + lastVcellRead + powerSource + multi-tick advance with state transition; TestStateFileAtomicRename x1 verifies os.replace wrapped + .tmp sibling source under same parent + canonical destination + .tmp gone post-rename; TestStateFileFailureIsolation x2 proves PermissionError graceful + missing-dir log+skip). MID-SESSION CORRECTION worth flagging: my first pass auto-created the parent dir via mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) which violated stop-condition #1 (US-277 owns dir creation). Caught on re-read of stop-condition spec text BEFORE shipping; removed the mkdir + rewrote test_missingParentDir_doesNotCrashTick to assert log+skip semantics (NO directory created + NO file written + ERROR logged + tick advances). Cost ~5 min; cost if shipped without re-read = Sprint-24 follow-up + Spool review note. Schema correctness audit: scripts/drain_forensics.py::_readOrchestratorState reads .get('pd_stage') and .get('pd_tick_count') -- those two keys are load-bearing per stop-condition #2 and the writer emits them verbatim (state.value enum string + int counter); Spool's spec'd additional fields (lastTickTimestamp via utcIsoNow per Sprint 14 US-202 standard, lastVcellRead, powerSource) are additive forensics not consumed by today's reader but trivially extensible. Remaining unclaimed 7/9: TIER 1-A US-277 (S; deploy-pi.sh auto-wire systemd unit + PYTHONPATH bonus -- the LAST TIER 1-A story; finishes the post-Drain-8 stack); TIER 1-B US-270 (TD-001 TestDataManager) + US-271 (TD-006 .env.example doc) + US-272 (TD-040 shape-not-literal) + US-273 (records hygiene I-015/016/017) + US-278 (UPS HAT dropout doc); TIER 2 US-274 (sprint_lint file-existence check). Alternatives: US-272 (S; one-liner shape-not-literal swap mirroring US-269 ~10 min); US-271 (S; .env.example doc-only addition ~5 min). PATTERN CARRYFORWARD: try/finally wrapping a state-machine body to guarantee a side-channel writes on EVERY return path -- reusable for any state machine where the body has multiple early-returns and a forensic side-channel must run regardless of the exit path; helper extraction (_tickBody) keeps the original body byte-identical and confines the wrap to the smallest necessary surface. PATTERN DISCOVERED: schema-alignment direction matters -- when wiring a producer/consumer pair, the side that ships first owns the schema; the side wiring later conforms (stop-condition #2 codified this for US-276). PATTERN REINFORCED: re-read stop-conditions BEFORE finalizing the implementation; my mid-session mkdir correction would have shipped if I hadn't re-read. PATTERN OBSERVED: opt-in default-None parameters preserve heavily-tested class fixtures without requiring sweeping test updates -- same pattern US-252 used for powerLogWriter; reusable for any side-channel addition to a class with many existing test sites.

----- PRIOR SESSION 142 -----
Session 142 -- US-275 SHIPPED passes:true (Sprint 23 TIER 1-A lead story; Round 3 INFO-level discriminator instrumentation -- the post-Drain-7 verdict-driven ladder discriminator now wired ahead of Drain Test 8). TWO artifacts: src/pi/power/orchestrator.py UPDATE (priorState capture at tick() entry; new _logTickDecision helper renders Spool's spec'd shape `vcell=%.3f currentStage=%s thresholds={WARNING:%.2f, IMMINENT:%.2f, TRIGGER:%.2f} willTransition=%s reason=%s`; INFO log emitted at 5 decision points -- (1) TRIGGER terminal -> reason=already_at_stage; (2) UNKNOWN source -> reason=power_source!=BATTERY; (3) NEW defensive None-guard on currentVcell -> reason=vcell_none; (4) IMMINENT->TRIGGER transition -> reason=OK with willTransition=True; (5) end-of-decision log on remaining BATTERY ticks computes willTransition=(state!=priorState) and emits reason=OK | threshold_not_crossed; AC happy path EXTERNAL-during-NORMAL preserves DEBUG bail and emits NO INFO log per spam-control invariant; AC restore EXTERNAL-during-non-NORMAL skips US-275 INFO because _acRestore has its own dedicated INFO line; enabled=False bail emits NO INFO because config-disabled is intentional not diagnostic; mod-history entry; vcell rendered as 'None' literal when None-guard fires; +56 lines incl docstring expansion -- now 803 LOC total), tests/pi/power/test_tick_internal_instrumentation.py NEW (488 lines / 6 tests across 2 classes -- TestTickDecisionLogPerReasonValue parametrized one-per-reason discriminator covering all 5 reason values + TestAcHappyPathEmitsNoInfoLog negative-invariant; mock fixture pattern mirrors US-266 test_tick_gating_no_silent_bail.py: real UpsMonitor wired to _MockI2cClient with scriptable VCELL/SOC reads, real byte-swap path so encoding bugs would surface). Pre-flight audit clear: rg `def tick|logger.info.*tick|currentStage|willTransition` src/pi/power/orchestrator.py confirmed zero existing INFO-level per-tick log (only US-266 DEBUG bail logs and stage-entry WARNING logs). DESIGN INSIGHT (carryforward): the `priorState` snapshot at tick() entry + post-tick `state != priorState` comparison is a clean way to compute `willTransition` without restructuring tick()'s sequential if-checks. 5 reason values map cleanly to Spool's discriminator hypotheses: power_source!=BATTERY = state-caching bug; vcell_none = upstream None-corruption (NEW defensive guard -- previously TypeError'd loud and was caught at hardware_manager._powerDownTickLoop's except Exception as ERROR); already_at_stage = TRIGGER terminal idempotence; threshold_not_crossed = comparison-logic bug (sign error/units mismatch); OK = transition fired (if no STAGE_* row post-OK then bug is downstream of tick(), US-267 fsync territory). Verification: pre-flight pytest tests/pi/power/test_tick_internal_instrumentation.py -v -- 5 reason tests FAILED + AC-no-spam PASSED in 60.36s; vcell_none case raised the EXACT TypeError mode the spec calls out (`'<=' not supported between instances of 'NoneType' and 'float'` at orchestrator.py:453). Post-fix: 6/6 PASS in 50.97s. Neighbor regression subset pi/{power,hardware,diagnostics,integration,obdii}: 834 passed / 12 skipped / 0 failures in 196.82s. Full fast suite: 3876 passed / 1 failed / 17 skipped / 19 deselected in 950.10s (15:50). Baseline post-US-269 (Session 141) was 3870 pass / 1 fail; delta +6 pass = exactly the 6 new tests + ZERO regressions. Residual 1 failure is pre-existing B-044 lint violation in scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py:80,332,333 (already on main; flagged Session 139-141; OUT OF SCOPE per Refusal Rule 3). Sprint-23-own-sprint failures: ZERO. ruff check src/pi/power/orchestrator.py tests/pi/power/test_tick_internal_instrumentation.py -- All checks passed! sprint_lint clean: 0 errors / 0 warnings across all 9 Sprint 23 stories (cleanest groom yet). Phantom-path drift: scope.filesToTouch listed real existing paths -- 11-session phantom-path-drift pattern remains broken (was 10-session at US-269). Pattern carryforward: paired-log-strategy where US-266 DEBUG covers per-bail forensic detail and US-275 INFO covers per-decision summary; the two are complementary not duplicative. Spool's post-Drain-8 verdict will read: if `reason=power_source!=BATTERY` fires repeatedly while we're clearly on battery -> state-caching bug; if `reason=threshold_not_crossed` fires while VCELL is clearly below WARNING -> comparison-logic bug; if `reason=vcell_none` fires -> upstream None-corruption; if `reason=OK` fires repeatedly but no STAGE_* row in power_log -> bug is in _enterStage/_writePowerLogStage (US-267 territory). Sprint 23 TIER 1-A status: 1/3 shipped (US-275 done; US-276 + US-277 unclaimed). Recommended next-iteration pick: US-276 (S; PowerDownOrchestrator state-file writer for /var/run/eclipse-obd/orchestrator-state.json -- the Sprint-22 ship-gap that left logger CSV's pd_stage and pd_tick_count columns at -1 across Drains 6+7; same orchestrator.py file as US-275 so context is warm; complements US-275 by giving Spool a CSV-side trail alongside the journalctl log). Alternative pick: US-277 (S; deploy-pi.sh auto-wire of drain-forensics systemd unit + PYTHONPATH bonus fix -- different file (deploy/deploy-pi.sh + deploy/drain-forensics.service) so independent of orchestrator.py work). Either choice unblocks Drain Test 8 with the full Sprint 23 TIER 1-A stack deployed.
